title: Hybrid roster discovery in JS is structurally impossible under file://
body: Dashboard avoids fetch() per existing index.html pattern. JS cannot read personas/{review,plan,check}/*.md at render time. Fix in /plan: have compute-persona-value.py walk personas/ and emit a sibling persona-roster.js (or extend dashboard-bundle.sh).

title: A4 doesn't assert contributing_finding_ids[] cleared on persona-prompt change
body: Pre-edit findings could persist in drill-down after window reset; add one-line assertion.

title: Freshness race check is insufficient — last_seen comparison can drop valid updates
body: 'Compare last_seen of new vs old; if older, abort' can drop valid updates. Different gates have different max timestamps; same last_seen can mean different content. Fix: lock file, OR compare a deterministic source high-water mark per project/gate. OR document last-writer-wins explicitly with no false-confidence freshness check.

title: Content-hash reset not implementable from current-state alone — weaken e2/A4
body: Persona prompt edits reset (persona, gate) window via persona_content_hash. But historical findings.jsonl rows don't record dispatch-time hash; compute script only knows current hash. A4 implies system can drop pre-edit data after one fresh dispatch — no boundary marker exists. Fix: weaken e2/A4 — current row gets new hash; historical attribution best-effort; old data may persist transiently until window rolls out.

title: No Project Discovery telemetry — adopters won't know what was found
body: One-line stderr summary of discovered projects would prevent a wave of adopter 'where's my data' questions.

title: XDG_CONFIG_HOME respect for tier-1 config path
body: ~/.config/monsterflow/projects should respect $XDG_CONFIG_HOME when set, not hardcode ~/.config.

title: Totals without averages awkward for cost ranking — add avg_tokens_per_invocation
body: Spec says dashboard divides if it wants, but dashboard columns only list total_tokens. Ranking 'lowest total cost' penalizes frequently used personas and rewards rarely used ones. Fix: include avg_tokens_per_invocation as first-class derived field, or require dashboard/wrap text to rank cost by average with total shown separately.

title: A1.5 disagreement branch lacks forcing function
body: Spec says 'if disagree, subagent transcript is canonical' but doesn't say who flips the implementation switch. Suggest 'On disagreement, A1.5 fails the build and /plan re-opens Open Q1.'

title: Multi-machine sync gap — JSONL gitignored, per-machine windows diverge
body: Same user on two machines gets different windows because JSONL doesn't sync. State the semantics explicitly.

title: Project Discovery is over-broad for public release — flip to opt-in
body: Auto-discovery via ~/Projects/*/docs/specs/ will scan private repos, forks, experiments by default. For public release the safer default is explicit opt-in. Concrete fix: default to current-repo-only + ~/.config/monsterflow/projects; make ~/Projects/* scanning opt-in via flag or config. Convergent with Stakeholders R3 finding on adopter-with-private-projects consent.

title: Persona-author exposure via rendered output (carryover) — screenshots leak names
body: Files-on-disk gate is closed (gitignore + leakage canary), but the rendered dashboard tab and /wrap-insights text section are share-able surfaces the Privacy section doesn't address. Screenshots leak persona names + survival rates outside the gitignored JSONL. Cheap mitigations: warning banner in tab, opt-in anonymization flag, or strip the bottom-3 list from text output.

title: contributing_finding_ids[] no length cap — soft-cap for public release
body: ~450KB total at upper bound. Soft-cap (e.g., last 50 + truncated_count) for the public-release case.

title: Pro-tier-friend commitment unenforceable — add A12 (v1.1 spec exists within 14 days)
body: 'Immediately after this lands' has no acceptance teeth. Suggest A12: docs/specs/account-type-scaling/spec.md exists within 14 days of v1 merge.

title: A11 outcome criterion underspecified at boundary — ground to findings.jsonl distinct pairs
body: '≥1 row per (persona, gate) pair seen in those runs' is fuzzier than necessary; ground to findings.jsonl distinct pairs rather than 'runs' so a crashed mid-dispatch doesn't false-fail.

title: Schema example lacks denominator annotations — readers misinterpret downstream<judge
body: Two survival rates have different denominators, not annotated in the schema example. Recommend inline JSONC comments distinguishing /total_emitted vs /total_judge_survived so dashboard readers don't misinterpret.

title: No row-TTL / cleanup story — JSONL grows unbounded over years
body: e7 covers personas-in-window only. Need a cleanup policy for very-old rows or an explicit decision to retain everything.

title: Run states unspecified — runs_in_window denominator ambiguous
body: If Agent dispatch exists but Judge failed / findings malformed / survival missing / /wrap-insights never ran for that gate — does that invocation count in runs_in_window? Spec implies yes for cost, but value denominators depend on artifacts that may not exist. Will bias survival upward (failed runs skipped) or unpredictably (cost-only runs count with zero emitted). Fix: add run_state column (complete_value, missing_raw, missing_findings, missing_survival, malformed, cost_only) and define which states count toward each denominator.

title: Three render surfaces is one too many — drop /wrap-insights ranking bare-arg
body: Dashboard tab + /wrap-insights top/bottom-3 sub-section + /wrap-insights ranking bare-arg full-table — drop the bare-arg full-table from v1; it duplicates the dashboard tab in ASCII.

title: Privacy tests too shallow — replace canary with allowlist schema
body: A10 only canary-checks for 'LEAKAGE_CANARY' string + a few field-name patterns (prompt, body, text, content). Misses leakage through file paths, user/project names in session IDs, descriptive timestamps, model metadata, persona names from private workflows, or arbitrary nested fields. contributing_finding_ids[] being 'sha256-derived' doesn't guarantee privacy if hash input is guessable. Fix: rewrite A10 as allowlist schema (schemas/persona-rankings.allowlist.json); tests reject every field not in allowlist.

title: A8 idempotency needs sort_keys=True in json.dumps
body: round(x, 6) is byte-stable (verified), but dict insertion order varies with glob order. Pin via sort_keys=True.

title: Worktree → Project Discovery dedup policy
body: Open Q2 leaks into discovery — phantom/duplicate projects from worktrees. Pre-commit policy now (Feasibility verified worktrees get own top-level dir; just dedupe by path).

title: No schema_version field on row — additive evolution to v1.1 will be lossy
body: JSONL row needs schema_version field for forward compatibility against the pinned idempotency allowlist.

title: Tier-1 config file lifecycle missing — who creates, missing-path behavior, discovery
body: Adopter onboarding gap. Document who creates ~/.config/monsterflow/projects, behavior on missing-path entries, and how adopters learn it exists.

title: Bullet counting is compression ratio, not survival — rename judge_survival_rate → judge_retention_ratio
body: Counting raw bullets assumes stable markdown output and treats Observations equal to Critical Gaps. More importantly, Judge can merge multiple raw bullets into one finding or split one bullet into multiple findings. So judge_survival_rate = findings/bullets is not a true survival rate — it's a compression ratio. Concrete fix: rename to judge_retention_ratio; update docs everywhere. Downstream-survival metric is unaffected.

title: A0 redaction script borderline meta-spec — bound as single-purpose
body: Bound it in one sentence in §Files created ('single-purpose; not a general-purpose redaction tool') to kill future scope-creep arguments.

title: survival.jsonl schema has no 'kept' enum value — drop from spec
body: Spec's filter outcome ∈ {addressed, kept} reduces to outcome == 'addressed' because survival.jsonl schema only has addressed, not_addressed, rejected_intentionally, classifier_error. Fix: drop 'kept' from spec (one-line) or extend the schema (heavier).

title: Null-rate sort position unspecified — pick: nulls always to bottom
body: e10/e11 + A5 say nulls render as '—' but don't say where they sort (top/bottom). Pick one (recommend: nulls always sort to bottom).

title: Cost/value join still under-specified at artifact level
body: Spec windows over (persona, gate) invocations from Agent dispatches, but value side comes from findings.jsonl/survival.jsonl which don't carry stable agent_tool_use_id. Re-runs of the same gate, retries, renamed specs, or overwritten stage directories conflate. Concrete fix: either declare v1 as 'best-effort aggregate by artifact directory' (smallest change) OR add agent_tool_use_id to findings-emit (bigger).

title: run.json location not defined in this spec — last_seen sourcing depends on it
body: last_seen sources from run.json.created_at, but spec doesn't pin run.json's path. If older runs lack run.json or multiple gates share one run file, ordering the 45-window becomes ambiguous. Fix: cite docs/specs/<feature>/<stage>/run.json (which persona-metrics directive already creates); specify fallback behavior for missing files.

title: Persona contributors have no doc on data lifecycle — risk of self-rejection
body: Contributor sees 20% rate from 5 runs and self-rejects PR. Cheap fix: one paragraph explaining insufficient-sample → window-stabilizes-at-45.

title: A11 fresh-install degradation — '≥10 historical runs' unsatisfiable on day 1
body: A11 outcome criterion 'after first /wrap-insights run on a project with ≥10 historical gate runs' is unsatisfiable for fresh adopters. Add one line specifying the empty-state behavior so they don't file a bug.

title: Downstream survival timing unspecified — pending_downstream state needed
body: Spec includes plan/check/code as possible downstream artifacts, but only defines a join through survival.jsonl. Doesn't say when survival is considered final, how delayed downstream artifacts update previous rows, or whether different gates have comparable survival semantics. Low downstream survival may just mean 'not evaluated yet.' Fix: add survival_artifact_type, survival_observed_at, pending_downstream state.
